epic.title: Communications, reminders & recall
epic.summary: The engine that keeps the book full and clients coming back at the right cadence: reminders, pre-/after-care sequences, and recall (~12-week toxin re-care) over SMS/email/push — with Spam Act consent/unsubscribe baked in. Advertising content is produced in the clinic's external tools; the platform has no advertising linter, and the public booking page uses generic names with S4 prices withheld by configuration.

stories.title: Notification channels (SMS / email / push)
stories.context: An INotifier port over an AU SMS provider + email + app push, with per-tenant templates (REQ-NOTIF-1, ADR-0012).
stories.i_want: a notification abstraction over SMS, email and push with per-tenant templates
stories.so_that: all messaging is consistent and provider-swappable
acceptance: INotifier supports SMS (AU provider), email and app push.
Per-tenant message templates supported.
Provider is swappable behind the port.
All sends log to the client's comms history.

stories.title: Reminders, confirmations & care sequences
stories.context: Appointment reminders/confirmations plus pre-care and aftercare sequences (multi-touch, timed per treatment type) (REQ-NOTIF-2). Transactional messages are exempt from opt-in.
stories.i_want: timely reminders I can confirm/decline and pre-/after-care instructions for my treatment
stories.so_that: I'm prepared and cared for around my visit
acceptance: Appointment reminders/confirmations send per template; confirm/decline updates the appointment (PRD-02).
Pre-care + aftercare sequences are multi-touch and timed per treatment type.
Transactional messages send regardless of marketing opt-in and avoid S4 references.
Sends are logged to comms history.

stories.title: Recall / recare worklist
stories.context: Recare at the treatment interval (toxin ~12 weeks) + unbooked-rebook prompts, with a recall worklist for front desk (REQ-NOTIF-3).
stories.i_want: a recall worklist of clients due to rebook and automatic recall nudges at the treatment interval
stories.so_that: clients return on cadence and the book stays full
acceptance: A toxin client with no future booking enters the recall worklist at the configured interval and receives the nudge.
Unbooked recommended sessions prompt a rebook.
Front desk can work the recall/rebook worklist.
Recall integrates with treatment plans (PRD-05) and rebooking (PRD-06).

stories.title: Marketing consent & functional unsubscribe (Spam Act)
stories.context: Opt-in for commercial electronic messages, sender identification and a functional unsubscribe that suppresses immediately on withdrawal (REQ-NOTIF-5, C23).
stories.i_want: to only receive marketing I opted into, with a working unsubscribe on every message
stories.so_that: my consent is respected per the Spam Act
acceptance: Marketing sends only to opted-in clients and always include a working unsubscribe.
Unsubscribing suppresses future marketing immediately.
Sender identification is included.
Suppression list is honoured across channels (and by PRD-06 reward comms).

stories.title: Public booking page: generic names, S4 prices withheld
stories.context: The public booking page uses generic service names and withholds S4 prices by configuration (catalog schedule flag), with no advertising linter (REQ-NOTIF-12, C9).
stories.i_want: my public booking page to use generic service names and withhold S4 prices automatically
stories.so_that: we don't reference S4 in public advertising
acceptance: The booking page renders generic service names and withholds S4 prices for any S4-flagged service (configuration-driven).
No brands, 'anti-wrinkle injections', prices or #botox appear for S4 services.
Configuration is driven by the catalog schedule flag (PRD-04).
Advertising compliance beyond this is clinic-owned (external tools).

stories.title: Unified follow-up / job queue
stories.context: Scattered recall / needs-attention / unanswered-comms items merge into one queue; staff can flag any message; inbound comms auto-categorise into jobs (rules/keyword, no AI) (REQ-NOTIF-7, ADR-0023).
stories.i_want: one follow-up queue that merges recalls, needs-attention items and flagged messages
acceptance: Recall, needs-attention and unanswered-comms items merge into a single queue.
Any message can be flagged so it isn't lost.
Inbound comms auto-categorise into jobs by rules/keyword (no AI).
A no-show (PRD-02) and negative reviews raise jobs into this queue.

stories.title: Omnichannel inbox + lead/reviews (placeholder)
stories.context: The omnichannel inbox (IG/FB/SMS/email), lead/prospect CRM and reviews/reputation are scoped to Phase 2; marketing DMs are out (Meta 24-h window). Placeholder (REQ-NOTIF-6/8/9, ADR-0018/0019/0032/0033).
stories.i_want: a unified inbox with lead tracking and review management
stories.so_that: all client conversations live in one place
acceptance: Placeholder — Phase 2; IG/FB/WhatsApp are reactive/service channels only (no cold-DM marketing).
Reviews acknowledge/flag/auto-detect-follow-up and the lead CRM are captured for later.
Meta feasibility (App Review, Business Verification, 24-h window) flagged for validation.
